ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE
Off Calendar Jan-29-2026 Off Calendar

ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE
Off Calendar Dec-24-2025 Continued To Feb-10-26 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE
Off Calendar Oct-17-2025 Continued To Dec-30-25 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

DEMURRER to COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Sep-04-2025 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

Defendant City and County of San Francisco's unopposed demurrer to the complaint is SUSTAINED WITH LEAVE TO AMEND.

Defendant's demurrer is based on Code of Civil Procedure sections 430.10(e) and 430.10(f). The issue under section 430.10(e) is, taking the facts properly pleaded and properly noticed as true, does the challenged cause of action necessarily fail to state a cause for relief. (Code of Civil Procedure section 430.10(e); Blank v. Kirwan (1985) 39 Cal.3d 311, 318.) In assessing whether the complaint states a cause of action, the court accepts all properly pleaded material facts, but not contentions, deductions, or conclusions of fact or law. (Minton v. Dignity Health (2019), 39 Cal.App.5th 1155, 1161.) "[I]f, on consideration of all facts stated, it appears that plaintiff is entitled to any relief against defendant, the complaint will be held good, though facts may not be clearly stated, or may be intermingled with a statement of other facts irrelevant to cause of action shown, or though plaintiff may demand relief to which he is not entitled under facts alleged." (Augustine v. Trucco (1954) 124 Cal.App.2d 229, 236, quoting Matteson v. Wagoner (1905) 147 Cal. 739, 742.) The court liberally construes the complaint per Code of Civil Procedure section 452.

Demurs for uncertainty under section 430.10(f) are disfavored and are granted only if the pleading is so incomprehensible that a defendant cannot reasonably respond. (Morris v. JP Morgan Chase Bank, N.A. (2022) 78 Cal.App.5th 279, 292.) Here, the court does not find the complaint fails for uncertainty. The allegations about the ambulance's conduct provide sufficient notice for King to respond.

Here, Plaintiff Timaima Yabaki's complaint fails to allege even minimal facts that would allow Defendant to reasonably respond. Thus, the complaint fails for uncertainty.

Moreover, Plaintiff has not alleged facts demonstrating or excusing compliance with the Government Claims Act. Defendant is a public entity. In order to allege a claim for money or damage against a public agency, a plaintiff must allege facts demonstrating or excusing compliance with claim requirements of the Government Claims Act. (See Government Code section 900 et seq.) Here, Plaintiff seeks money and/or damages but has not alleged facts demonstrating or excusing compliance with claim requirements of the Government Claims Act. Absent such allegations, Plaintiff fails to allege a claim upon which relief can be granted.

The court grants Plaintiff 20 days to file an amended complaint that, at a minimum, allege facts (1) sufficient to state a claim to which Defendant can reasonably be expected to respond and (2) demonstrating or excusing compliance with the Government Claims Act.

Defendant's request for judicial notice is denied. The court granted the demurrer without considering the facts of which notice was sought.

Order is signed.

Judge: Joseph M. Quinn; Clerk: M. Macadangdang; Not Reported. (302/JMQ)
